factors.name: SpaceX IPO mega-event
factors.summary: SpaceX closed +19% on Friday after $75B IPO, becoming 6th most-valuable US company and turning Musk into world's first trillionaire (Bloomberg). This is a watershed moment for mega-cap tech sentiment and risk appetite.

factors.name: Anthropic AI model shutdown (government directive)
factors.summary: US government ordered Anthropic to pull Fable and Mythos models citing national security/Amazon concerns (CoinDesk, Decrypt, Cointelegraph 2026-06-13). Anthropic pre-IPO shares fell sharply. This signals AI regulation risk and potential upcoming regulatory headwinds for megacaps.

factors.name: Iran peace deal near (geopolitical risk premium relief)
factors.summary: Trump posted deal will be signed Sunday; Pakistan said accord could finalize within 24h (Bloomberg, All News 2026-06-13). Strait of Hormuz reopening would ease energy costs and inflation expectations—positive for growth-heavy Nasdaq.

factors.name: Memory chip shortage narrative (AI capex bottleneck)
factors.summary: CoinDesk and stock news flagged memory shortage escalation and AI-driven memory bottleneck (All News 2026-06-13). This threatens semiconductor supply chain and hyperscaler capex efficiency—critical for Nvidia, TSMC, and broader NDX valuation.

factors.name: Fed/BOE guarded stance post-Iran war (100 days)
factors.summary: Bloomberg Markets reports central banks still weighing inflation vs. growth risk from Iran conflict (2026-06-13 20:00). No new hawkish/dovish signal yet; uncertainty on rates persists.

factors.name: Social Security insolvency concerns (long-term fiscal headwind)
factors.summary: Multiple MarketWatch pieces highlight 22% benefit cliff in 2032, national debt now $1M per household, and 400% of GDP (2026-06-13 19:00). Structural fiscal stress could pressure equity multiples long-term, though not immediate catalyst.

factors.name: Inflation signals mixed (COLA 4.7% potential for 2027)
factors.summary: MarketWatch reports Social Security COLA could hit 4.7% in 2027 as inflation hits 3-year high (2026-06-13 19:00). No fresh CPI/PCE data in this window; inflation narrative remains uncertain.

factors.name: Google AI/Search makeover + Gemini phishing abuse
factors.summary: Google getting AI makeover for search (positive product narrative), but also sued by Chinese crime group for Gemini misuse in mass phishing scams (Decrypt 2026-06-13 16:01). Regulatory/safety risk vs. product momentum tension.

factors.name: Price action: 4h -0.22%, 24h +0.46%
factors.summary: Minimal intraday weakness after weekend news, slight 24h gain. Price action shows no extreme momentum in either direction—consistent with choppy pre-event period or consolidation.

factors.name: Antitrust / regulatory risk (AI models, Google, Amazon)
factors.summary: Anthropic model ban, Amazon reportedly triggered Anthropic restrictions, Google phishing lawsuit—regulatory scrutiny on big tech and AI is elevated. Could pressure mega-cap valuations.

screening.narrative: Nasdaq faces a classic risk-on/risk-off crosscurrent. SpaceX's record IPO and potential Iran peace deal fuel sentiment and remove geopolitical premium, supporting equities. However, Anthropic's forced model shutdown and rising regulatory scrutiny on AI, memory chip supply bottlenecks, and long-term fiscal deterioration create offsetting headwinds. Price action is flat (±0.5% in 24h), suggesting the market is holding its breath heading into multiple catalysts: Iran deal finalization (Sunday), upcoming mega-cap earnings, and potential Fed/inflation data.
catalysts_ahead: Trump announces Iran deal signing (expected Sunday, 2026-06-15)
Strait of Hormuz reopening negotiations (24-48h)
Memory chip supply data and AI capex guidance from hyperscalers
Next CPI/PCE print (likely week of 2026-06-17+)
Mega-cap earnings guidance (AAPL, MSFT, GOOGL, NVDA may release next week)
Fed speakers / rate expectations reset
Further Anthropic or AI regulation announcements
screening.risk_warning: Iran deal falls through or escalates → spike in oil/inflation expectations, repricing real yields higher, would crush long-duration tech valuations. Conversely, dovish Fed surprise or cool inflation data would lift NAS100 above 30,000 quickly. Regulatory escalation (antitrust actions, AI bans affecting NVDA or GOOGL capex) could trigger 1-2% selloff. Memory shortage deepens → NVDA guidance miss → sector capitulation. Current NEUTRAL stance assumes equilibrium; any catalyst tipping toward either side will shift bias sharply by 2-3 confidence points.
screening.entry_guidance: Market is in consolidation with mixed signals. Wait for Iran deal confirmation (yes/no) and next inflation data (CPI/PCE) before committing. If deal signed + inflation cools, buy breakout above 29,850; if deal fails or inflation hot, short pullback to 29,400. Avoid chasing from current levels—early Phase 1, but catalyst risk is elevated.

factors.name: SpaceX IPO Catalyst
factors.summary: SpaceX closed +19% on first trading day (Friday 6/13), becoming 6th most-valuable US company at ~$2T valuation. Historic mega-cap IPO success wiped away prior week's anxiety and boosted broad tech sentiment.

factors.name: AI Capex & Hyperscaler Demand
factors.summary: Goldman Sachs analyzing impact of AI capex boom on S&P 500 ROE; memory chip shortage concerns emerging but demand backdrop remains robust. AI infrastructure investment narrative continues to support mega-cap growth multiples.

factors.name: Geopolitical Risk: Iran War / Strait of Hormuz
factors.summary: US-Iran deal could be signed 'Sunday' per Trump (6/13 timing still uncertain per Tehran); Pakistan said deal could finalize within 24h. Ongoing 100-day Iran war creating inflation/growth uncertainty that ECB's Nagel says will keep prices elevated longer even if conflict ends.

factors.name: AI Regulation / Government Crackdown
factors.summary: Trump administration suspended foreign access to Anthropic's Fable 5 and Mythos 5 models following Amazon/tech firm warnings (6/13). Google sued Chinese crime group for weaponizing Gemini AI. Regulatory friction emerging in AI space could dampen capex euphoria.

factors.name: Dollar & Real Yields
factors.summary: Dollar likely to find support if Strait remains closed (geopolitical premium). No major inflation print or Fed data in past 24h; real yields not yet re-priced materially. Awaiting next CPI/PCE release for clarity on rate path.

factors.name: Recent Price Action: Intraday Weakness
factors.summary: NAS100 down -0.22% in 4h window despite +0.46% 24h gain. SpaceX IPO bounce appears to be fading into short-term chop; late Friday strength not holding into weekend open.

factors.name: Macro Debt & Fiscal Pressure
factors.summary: MarketWatch reports US effective national debt hit $100T (400% of GDP) for first time; Social Security facing 22% cliff in 2032. Structural fiscal headwinds weigh on long-duration growth multiples, though not immediate catalyst.

factors.name: Mega-cap Earnings Calendar
factors.summary: No major AAPL, MSFT, GOOGL, AMZN, NVDA earnings announced in news window. Earnings season not front-and-center; next catalyst typically CPI or Fed speakers.

factors.name: South Korea MSCI Upgrade Momentum
factors.summary: KOSPI approaching developed-market status upgrade at MSCI; Citi raised KOSPI target to 10,000. Emerging market / growth rotation tailwinds support broad risk-on, though secondary to US tech.

factors.name: Crypto Risk-On Correlation
factors.summary: Bitcoin rose above $64k on Iran peace deal rumors (6/13 via Pakistan PM); SpaceX IPO + broader risk sentiment supporting crypto bid. Risk-on correlations intact; NQ-BTC linkage supports near-term upside if geopolitical risk cools.

screening.narrative: NAS100 bounced +0.46% over 24h following SpaceX's historic +19% IPO debut on Friday, which temporarily cleared a week of Iran-war anxiety and re-ignited mega-cap tech euphoria. However, intraday weakness (-0.22% in 4h) suggests the bounce is fading into weekend chop, and multiple cross-currents remain unresolved: geopolitical risk (Iran deal timing still uncertain), emerging AI regulation friction (Anthropic model restrictions, Google Gemini weaponization), and structural fiscal headwinds (debt/GDP at 400%, Social Security insolvency risk). Confidence is muted because the SpaceX catalyst has already inflated valuations sharply, while macro uncertainty (inflation, real yields, rate path) lacks fresh data points to confirm a sustained rally.
catalysts_ahead: US-Iran deal signing timing (Trump said 'Sunday' 6/16, but Tehran disputes; commodity/FX volatility if deal confirmed or collapses)
CPI / PCE inflation print (next window; critical for Fed rate expectations and growth multiple repricing)
FOMC speaker commentary or Fed policy signals (impact on real yields and long-duration tech multiples)
Earnings announcements from AAPL, MSFT, GOOGL, NVDA (single mega-caps can move NAS100 2-3%)
South Korea MSCI upgrade decision timeline (emerging market rotations could shift capital flows away from US mega-cap tech)
Antitrust / AI regulation developments (DOJ, FTC, EU DMA actions against big tech)
screening.risk_warning: SpaceX IPO success masked underlying fragility: geopolitical risk (Iran war, Strait of Hormuz closure) could reignite into broader risk-off if peace deal collapses. AI regulation crackdown by Trump administration contradicts capex-boom narrative and could undermine Anthropic/OpenAI IPO momentum. Structural fiscal concerns (debt spiral, Social Security insolvency) are not priced in near-term but could weigh on sentiment if CPI re-accelerates and Fed is forced to hold rates higher for longer. A material retest of support at 29,450 or breach below 29,000 would invalidate any bullish bias.
screening.entry_guidance: Bias is NEUTRAL; wait for either a confirmed Iran deal (risk-on reversal toward 29,800–30,000) or fresh inflation/Fed data to reset rate expectations. Avoid chasing the SpaceX bounce; if NAS100 holds above 29,500 into Monday open and Iran deal finalizes, then consider long entry near 29,600 with stop at 29,400. Alternatively, if geopolitical headlines deteriorate or CPI surprise hawkish, look for short entry on retest of 29,800 resistance.

factors.name: SpaceX IPO mega-catalyst
factors.summary: SpaceX closed +19% on Friday (6/13), with historic $75B IPO becoming 6th most-valuable US company. Musk now world's first trillionaire; sentiment tailwind for NQ-heavy Mag7 correlation and risk-on appetite.

factors.name: Crypto risk-on surge
factors.summary: Bitcoin topped $64,000+ on ETF inflows and Iran peace deal optimism. Strong NQ-BTC linkage on risk-on days; crypto strength typically precedes equity rallies in tech/growth.

factors.name: Iran peace deal narrative
factors.summary: Trump claims Sunday signing deadline; Pakistan PM reports imminent deal. Strait of Hormuz reopening would reduce geopolitical premium, reopen liquidity to risk assets and commodities.

factors.name: AI narrative resilience
factors.summary: Intel double-upgrade by analysts; Anthropic's Mythos AI validation in Zcash; SpaceX IPO hyper-valuations prove AI/moonshot optimism intact despite regulatory headwinds (Anthropic crackdown).

factors.name: Anthropic regulatory friction
factors.summary: US government orders Anthropic to pull Claude Fable/Mythos AI models; Amazon pressure prompted crackdown. Reflects growing AI regulation risk that could cap valuation expansion for Open AI, Anthropic successors.

factors.name: Macro fiscal concern
factors.summary: MarketWatch reports US national debt hit $1M per household and $100T notional (400% GDP). Social Security solvency cliff in 2032, inflation pressure (COLA +4.7% in 2027 forecast). Long-duration growth multiple headwind if real yields rise or inflation sticks.

factors.name: 4h/24h price action
factors.summary: 4h change -0.22%, 24h change +0.46%. Flat consolidation after Friday's SpaceX pop; no follow-through momentum yet into Monday open.

factors.name: Fed/rate policy absence
factors.summary: No major FOMC or macro data surprises in last 24h. No CPI/PCE/NFP prints noted in news window. Market operating on SpaceX/geopolitical optimism rather than Fed re-pricing.

factors.name: South Korea MSCI upgrade chatter
factors.summary: South Korea rally after volatile week; potential MSCI developed-market upgrade. Positive for global EM sentiment but indirect effect on NAS100.

screening.narrative: NAS100 is consolidating after Friday's SpaceX IPO sparked a broad risk-on sentiment surge and Musk became world's first trillionaire. The 4h/-0.22% and 24h/+0.46% readings suggest a dull Monday open holding gains, awaiting confirmation catalysts. Bullish underpinnings are SpaceX momentum, Bitcoin's break above $64K, and optimism around imminent Iran peace deal (Strait of Hormuz reopening narrative). However, macro headwinds—US fiscal deterioration, inflation stickiness, and nascent AI regulation (Anthropic crackdown)—cap upside conviction. Price action remains in Phase 1, with no major move extension yet; next 48-72h will test whether SpaceX IPO euphoria sustains into Wednesday/Thursday macro calendar.
catalysts_ahead: CPI/Core CPI print (if scheduled this week; would repruce Fed expectations and real yields)
Iran peace deal formal signature (Trump claims Sunday 6/16; geopolitical risk reset)
OpenAI, Anthropic IPO rumors / additional AI regulation headlines
NVDA, MSFT, GOOGL, AAPL earnings or forward guidance (if scheduled imminent)
Treasury yield moves on fiscal/inflation narrative (10y and TIPS real yields critical for NQ multiples)
VIX/VXN vol regime (currently not elevated; SpaceX IPO soaked up retail vol; watch for capitulation or reversion)
screening.risk_warning: Bias is invalidated if: (1) Iran deal talks collapse or escalate → geopolitical risk-off, VIX spike, flight to quality pressures equities. (2) US fiscal/inflation data surprises hot → real yields repriced upward, long-duration growth crushed. (3) SpaceX IPO enthusiasm fades into profit-taking within 48-72h, triggering broader NQ pullback. (4) Major antitrust or AI regulation action announced → Mag7 selloff. (5) BTC falls below $63K → loss of crypto momentum, NQ risk-on correlation breaks. Confidence capped at 6 because macro catalysts (CPI, rate expectations) remain dormant in current news window.
screening.entry_guidance: On strength: wait for retest of 29500–29600 support zone before adding longs; move has not extended into Phase 2 yet, so near-term dip buying on SpaceX momentum is lower-risk. On weakness: 29800–30000 resistance is next target; if price exceeds 29800, consider scaling in on breaks above SPX/NQ momentum. Avoid chasing into Friday close; prefer intraday pullbacks to 29500 as lower-risk entry if bullish thesis persists.

factors.name: SpaceX IPO sentiment surge
factors.summary: SpaceX closed +19% on historic $75B IPO debut (June 13), described as 'stick the landing' with moderate volatility despite huge retail allocation. This mega-cap tech win removes week of anxiety and boosts investor risk appetite toward growth/tech.

factors.name: Iran peace deal narrative (risk-on tailwind)
factors.summary: Trump announced Sunday signing of US-Iran deal to reopen Strait of Hormuz; Pakistan PM confirmed near-finalization. Crypto and risk assets rallied on this (BTC near $65K per CoinDesk). Geopolitical de-escalation reduces energy/inflation tail risks.

factors.name: AI capex and hyperscaler commentary
factors.summary: Goldman analyzing AI capex boom impact on S&P 500 ROE; SpaceX-Google partnership celebrating blockbuster week. No negative guidance on cloud capex or semiconductor demand visible in 24h window. Hyperscaler order trends remain constructive.

factors.name: Recent price action (24h/4h momentum)
factors.summary: NAS100 +0.46% over 24h but only -0.22% in last 4h, suggesting some profit-taking after SpaceX pop. Move is modest and not extended; no extreme momentum read.

factors.name: Fed/rates/real yields (data window)
factors.summary: No major inflation (CPI/PCE), FOMC, or Treasury yield moves reported in 24h news. Fed remains 'guarded' per Bloomberg (post-100-day Iran war context). No near-term macro shock to duration/valuation, but also no fresh dovish catalyst.

factors.name: Antitrust and AI regulation
factors.summary: Trump admin suspended Anthropic's Fable/Mythos AI models (June 13) following Amazon CEO pressure; Google sued for Gemini misuse in phishing scams. Regulatory overhang on mega-cap AI vendors (GOOGL, MSFT, AMZN) adds uncertainty.

factors.name: Tech earnings calendar (PHASE 2 prep)
factors.summary: No mega-cap earnings (AAPL, MSFT, NVDA, GOOGL, AMZN, META, TSLA) announced in this 24h window. Next catalyst likely early next week; analysts upgrading Intel post-AI momentum. Valuation support remains dependent on forward guidance.

factors.name: Volatility and options positioning
factors.summary: SpaceX IPO ran with 'moderate volatility' despite huge size; VIX/VXN not explicitly reported as elevated. Dealer gamma and single-name hedging in NVDA/AAPL/TSLA not flagged as a constraint.

factors.name: Dollar and international growth exposure
factors.summary: No FX or DXY moves cited in news. Iran peace deal could eventually weaken USD (risk-on), but no immediate headline on dollar weakness.

factors.name: Bias history shift (fresh read)
factors.summary: Previous reads (Jun 14 09:32, 05:48, 00:05) ranged NEUTRAL4-5 with no confirmation. Today's BUY6 reflects SpaceX IPO pop and Iran peace narrative — this is a FRESH CYCLE reaction, not a sustained multi-day setup.

screening.narrative: NAS100 broke higher overnight on SpaceX's blockbuster +19% IPO debut and geopolitical de-escalation (Iran peace deal Sunday). Risk sentiment improved sharply, lifting mega-cap tech and hyperscaler names. However, the 24h gain (+0.46%) is modest and the 4h action shows light profit-taking (-0.22%), suggesting the move is in PHASE 2 (consolidating after initial pop). No fresh macro data or mega-cap earnings catalysts in the news window; regulatory headwinds on AI (Anthropic model suspension, Google Gemini phishing) add noise. Confidence is moderate (6/10) because sentiment is constructive but the move lacks follow-through breadth and duration.
catalysts_ahead: FOMC decision / Powell press conference (if this week)
CPI/PCE inflation print (timing TBD)
NFP payrolls report
Mega-cap tech earnings: AAPL, MSFT, GOOGL, AMZN, NVDA, TSLA guidance
Iran peace deal formal signing (Sunday per Trump)
SpaceX options launch (expected soon, per news)
Anthropic / OpenAI regulatory developments
screening.risk_warning: A hot inflation surprise or hawkish Fed pivot would reverse this BUY bias and compress growth multiples, especially for long-duration Nasdaq names. Iran peace deal failure or new geopolitical flare-up would deflate risk-on. Antitrust escalation or AI regulation crackdowns on MSFT, GOOGL, AMZN could trigger single-name downside that pressures the index. Entry now risks chasing the SpaceX momentum pop — pullback to 29400-29500 support would be safer re-entry if bias holds.
screening.entry_guidance: Move is in PHASE 2 (post-catalyst, consolidating). Recommend waiting for retest of 29400-29500 support or a fresh catalyst (earnings, Fed decision) before adding longs. If chasing here, set stop at 29300 and target 29900-30000 into next week. Avoid size into earnings/data blackout.
